notes_global: Ground-truth structural inventory for the 5 pilot chapters that validate the automated knowledge-audit engine (>=85% agreement gate). Built by reading rag_content_chunks text from prod (grade_short/subject_code/chapter_number join; board=CBSE). Counts are evidence-based and reproducible from chunk text. CRITICAL corpus finding: 4 of 5 'richest' chapters are cross-chapter CONTAMINATED - the chunk store has merged multiple source PDFs under one syllabus row, so high chunk_count is NOT a proxy for single-chapter completeness. Chunks are also heavily OVERLAPPED (sliding-window re-chunking duplicates the same passage 2-3x), so all counts below are DISTINCT-deduplicated by identifier (figure number, example number, etc.), not raw occurrence counts. Where a dimension spans a native chapter plus a foreign contaminant, the 'count' reflects the native chapter's items where separable and 'notes' records the contamination; low-confidence dimensions are flagged in notes.
confidence_key: high = directly enumerable by stable identifier (Fig N.M / Example N.M / Table N.M / Activity N.M); medium = header/section text present but overlap or OCR noise inflates raw matches; low = contamination or OCR garbling makes an exact native-chapter count unreliable.

chapters.source_book: Curiosity | Textbook of Science | Grade 6 (new NCERT, Reprint 2026-27)
chapters.contamination: none (cleanest of the 5). Only artifact: book-title string 'Curiosity | Textbook of Science | Grade 6' captured into the concept metadata field, and heavy chunk overlap (each passage stored ~2x).

evidence: 2.3 	Plants and Animals in Different Surroundings
headings.notes: Numbered NCERT sections 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 (2.1-2.3 directly observed; 2.4 inferred from figure/table run). Confidence: medium (page-order scrambled by overlap).

topics.notes: Single topic value populated in the topic metadata column across all chunks. High confidence.

evidence: Plants and Animals in Different Surroundings
subtopics.notes: Equal to the numbered sections; no finer subtopic structure in this book. Medium confidence.

concepts.notes: corrected 2026-07-04: was chunk-metadata noise (2 = real topic + book-title string); true source = chapter_concepts SSoT count. Prod chapter_concepts has exactly 4 active rows for (grade 6, science, ch2): 'Diversity in Nature', 'Grouping of Plants', 'Adaptations in Plants and Animals', 'Habitats of Plants and Animals'. The engine now reads this deterministic COUNT(chapter_concepts) instead of enumerating from OCR-flattened chunk prose. High confidence.

definitions.notes: Informal 'is called' / 'are called' definitions (grouping, adaptation, habitat, amphibians, aquatic, terrestrial, dicots, monocots, biodiversity, venation). No formal 'X is defined as' phrasing in this book. ~12 raw 'is called' matches collapse to ~10 distinct concepts after dedup. Medium confidence.

formulae.notes: No mathematical formulae/equations in this biology chapter. High confidence.

examples.notes: This new-NCERT book uses no numbered 'Example N.M' worked blocks. High confidence (0).

evidence: 1. 	Here are two types of seeds. What differences...
exercises.notes: End-of-chapter question set titled 'Let us enhance our learning' (this book's name for Exercises). Questions 1-8 observed; numbering is out of order in the chunks (2,3,4 then 8,5,6,7) due to overlap, so 8 is a floor. Medium confidence.

evidence: Fig. 2.1: Examples of different features of some plants
diagrams.notes: Distinct figure numbers Fig. 2.1 through Fig. 2.13, contiguous (no gaps). High confidence.

evidence: Fig. 2.1: Examples of different features of some plants
Fig. 2.8: Body parts used by animals for movement
captions.notes: Every figure has a colon-delimited caption; captions match the 13 figures 1:1. High confidence.

evidence: We are surrounded by a large variety of plants and animals.
summary.notes: Present. 'Summary' heading with ~15 bullet points. present=yes, bullet_count~15. High confidence on presence, medium on exact bullet count.

evidence: Adaptation / Biodiversity / Cotyledon / Dicot plants / Habitat...
keywords.notes: Explicit 'Keywords' block with ~24 terms (Adaptation, Biodiversity, Cotyledon, Dicot plants, Habitat, Fibrous root, Herbs, Sacred groves, Venation, Monocot plants, Reticulate venation, Parallel venation, Taproot, Shrubs, Amphibians, Aquatic, Tree, Terrestrial, plus process verbs). Medium confidence (verb tokens Relate/Record/Observe interleaved). present=yes.

suspected_missing: Question 1 of 'Let us enhance our learning' appears only as an embedded sub-fragment; full q1 stem may be split across chunks.
Section 2.4 heading not directly captured though its figures/tables are present - possible mid-chapter truncation of a heading line.

chapters.source_book: MIXED: NCERT Class 9 Maths ch6 'Lines and Angles' (Reprint 2025-26) + a DIFFERENT book 'Ganita Manjari | Grade 9 | Part I' chapter 'Measuring Space: Perimeter and Area' (Chapter 6.indd, Reprint 2026-27)
chapters.contamination: SEVERE cross-chapter merge. Two unrelated 'Chapter 6' PDFs are stored under the same (grade 9, math, ch6) key: (a) Lines and Angles (geometry: intersecting lines, pairs of angles, parallel lines, Theorems 6.1/6.6) and (b) Perimeter and Area / Circles (mensuration: perimeter of a circle, C/D ratio, pi is irrational, arc length, area of rectangle/parallelogram/triangle). Figure/section/example numbering collides (both use 6.x), so distinct-by-number counts CANNOT separate the two chapters. Note fetched=76 rows, only 66 active (10 inactive chunks).

evidence: 6.5 Problems, Puzzles, and Paradoxes on Perimeter
headings.notes: Union of BOTH chapters' numbered sections: Lines&Angles {6.1 Introduction, 6.2 Basic Terms and Definitions, 6.3 Intersecting Lines..., 6.4 Pairs of Angles, 6.5 Lines Parallel to the Same Line} and Perimeter&Area {6.1 Perimeter of a Shape, 6.2 Perimeter of a Circle, 6.3 pi Is Irrational, 6.4 Length of an Arc, 6.5 Problems/Puzzles..., 6.6 Area of a Rectangle, 6.7 Area of a Parallelogram, 6.8 Area of a Triangle}. ~13 distinct section headings across the two. Low confidence for a single-chapter count.

topics.notes: topic metadata column uniformly 'Lines and Angles' even on the Perimeter/Area chunks - metadata does NOT reflect the contamination. High confidence on the value, but the value is wrong for ~half the chunks.

evidence: Length of an Arc of a Circle
subtopics.notes: Equals the merged section headings. Low confidence (contaminated).

concepts.notes: concept metadata column is populated with running page-header OCR ('70 mathematics', 'lines and angles 71', ...) - pure noise, not pedagogical concepts. Not usable as a concept count. Low confidence.

definitions.notes: ~22 raw 'is defined as/is called' matches across both chapters; ~15 distinct after dedup (angle types, complementary/supplementary, adjacent, linear pair, vertically opposite, transversal, plus perimeter/circumference/pi from the contaminant). Low confidence (spans two chapters).

formulae.notes: Native Lines&Angles has essentially no numbered formulae (angle-sum relations only). The Perimeter/Area contaminant contributes formulae (perimeter, circumference, pi approximations 22/7, 355/113, area of rectangle/parallelogram/triangle). ~6 distinct formulae, ALL from the contaminant. Low confidence.

evidence: Example 1 : In Fig. 6.9, lines PQ and RS
Example 6 : In Fig. 6.22, AB || CD and CD || EF
examples.notes: Lines&Angles worked Examples 1-6 (plain-numbered, geometry). The Perimeter/Area contaminant adds its own Example 1, Example 2 (perimeter/circle). ~8 distinct example blocks across both. Medium-low confidence; 6 are native Lines&Angles.

evidence: Example 2 : In Fig. 6.10, ray OS stands on a line POQ
Example 4 : In Fig. 6.19, if PQ || RS, angle MXQ = 135 deg
solved_examples.notes: All examples here are worked/solved (step-by-step proofs), so solved_examples == examples. Same contamination caveat. Medium-low confidence.

evidence: 1. In Fig. 6.13, lines AB and CD intersect at O...
exercises.notes: Native Lines&Angles: EXERCISE 6.1 (>=1 question captured), EXERCISE 6.2. Question-level count unreliable - only q1 of 6.1 clearly captured; NCERT ch6 has ~6 in 6.1 and ~6 in 6.2 but chunks truncate. 'exercise' markers total ~4-5 across both chapters. count=5 is a rough exercise-block/marker count, NOT a question count. Low confidence.

activities.notes: No 'Activity N.M' blocks (maths chapters use Examples/Exercises). High confidence (0).

tables.notes: No 'Table N.M' references detected in either chapter's chunks. High confidence (0).

diagrams.notes: 55 distinct figure numbers Fig. 6.1 through Fig. 6.55 - but this SPANS both merged chapters (geometry figures + perimeter/area/circle figures share the 6.x namespace and cannot be split by number). Native Lines&Angles alone is ~ Fig 6.1-6.16. Low confidence for single-chapter; high confidence for the raw distinct-number union.

captions.notes: Many figures are bare references ('Fig. 6.13') with no colon caption; only ~30 of 55 carry descriptive captions. Low confidence (contaminated + partial captioning).

summary.notes: A '6.6 Summary' heading is present - but it belongs to the Perimeter/Area contaminant, not Lines&Angles. Native Lines&Angles summary not clearly captured. present=yes(but-foreign). Low confidence.

keywords.notes: No explicit Keywords/Glossary block detected. High confidence (0).

suspected_missing: Native 'Lines and Angles' end-of-chapter summary appears absent (only the foreign Perimeter/Area '6.6 Summary' present).
EXERCISE 6.1 and 6.2 question stems are truncated - only q1 of 6.1 fully captured; remaining questions missing/split.
10 of 76 fetched chunks are is_active=false (excluded from the 66 active) - possible superseded/duplicate content.
ENTIRE FOREIGN CHAPTER ('Measuring Space: Perimeter and Area' from Ganita Manjari) is mis-filed here and should not be under Lines and Angles.

chapters.chapter_title: Notes For The Teacher Notes For The Teacher Notes For The Teacher Notes For The Teacher Notes For The Teacher
chapters.source_book: MIXED: at least 4 different Class 10 social-science books merged
chapters.contamination: SEVERE multi-book merge (the worst of the 5). The chapter_title itself is garbled OCR ('Notes For The Teacher' x5). The 148 chunks conflate at minimum: (1) Agriculture (Contemporary India II - Geography), (2) Globalisation and the Indian Economy (Understanding Economic Development - Economics), (3) Political Parties (Democratic Politics II - Civics), (4) India and the Contemporary World II (History). This is not a single chapter; it is a bag of chunks from ~4 chapters across 4 subjects, all filed under one (grade 10, social_studies, ch4) row. Per-dimension counts below are near-meaningless as single-chapter ground truth and are marked low confidence throughout.

evidence: G E N D E R , R E L I G I O N A N D C A S T E
headings.notes: Headings are heavily letter-spaced OCR ('P O L I T I C A L') and belong to multiple books; no coherent single-chapter heading hierarchy is recoverable. count=0 as a single-chapter structure (undeterminable). Low confidence.

evidence: notes for the teacher notes for the teacher notes for the teacher...
topics.notes: topic metadata column is the garbled 'Notes For The Teacher' string - useless. The real topics span Agriculture/Globalisation/Political Parties/History. Low confidence.

subtopics.notes: Not separable across the 4 merged books. Undeterminable. Low confidence.

concepts.notes: 46 distinct values in the concept metadata column, but they are a mix of running-header OCR ('32 Contemporary India - II'), page numbers ('79','46'), and occasional real phrases ('globalisation', 'small producers: compete or perish'). NOT a clean concept count - mostly noise + cross-book. Low confidence.

definitions.notes: ~30 raw 'is called/defined as/known as' matches; ~20 distinct after dedup, spanning economics (MNCs, globalisation, liberalisation, trade barrier, SEZ), geography (subsistence/commercial farming), civics (political party, alliance). Cross-book. Low confidence.

formulae.notes: No mathematical formulae (social science). High confidence (0).

examples.notes: No 'Example N.M' worked blocks (SST uses case studies/boxes, not numbered examples). High confidence (0).

evidence: 1 what do you understand by globalisation? explain in your own words.
exercises.notes: A single 'EXERCISES' block region detected (line ~3972). Questions belong to the Globalisation (Economics) chapter (e.g. 'What do you understand by globalisation?'). Because 4 chapters are merged, the native ch4 exercise set is indeterminate. count=1 exercise-block. Low confidence; question count not reliably extractable.

activities.notes: One 'additional activity / project' marker in the concept metadata. Low confidence.

tables.notes: No 'Table N.M' references detected. Medium confidence (SST data often in un-numbered boxes).

diagrams.notes: 16 distinct Fig. 4.x numbers (4.1-4.15, with 4.4a/4.4b split). But these span multiple merged books that each happen to use ch4 figure numbering - cannot attribute to one chapter. Low confidence for single-chapter.

captions.notes: Partial captioning; many figures are bare references. ~10 with descriptive captions. Low confidence.

summary.notes: No 'Summary'/'What you have learnt' heading detected across the merged chunks (summary_marker=0 in pre-scan). Notable given 148 chunks - likely truncated. present=no(detected). Medium confidence.

keywords.notes: No Keywords/Glossary block detected. Medium confidence (0).

suspected_missing: This row is NOT a single chapter - it merges ~4 chapters from 4 different Class 10 SST books (Geography/Economics/Civics/History). It should be split into 4 syllabus rows.
No end-of-chapter Summary detected despite 148 chunks.
chapter_title is garbled OCR ('Notes For The Teacher' repeated) - metadata drift/corruption.
Exercise question set is fragmentary and belongs to only one (Globalisation) of the 4 merged chapters.

chapters.chapter_title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
chapters.source_book: MIXED: NCERT Class 11 Physics ch6 'System of Particles and Rotational Motion' + foreign chapter numbered 13 (Oscillations / SHM content: springs, pendulum, damped/forced oscillations)
chapters.contamination: MODERATE-SEVERE. Native ch6 (rotational motion: centre of mass, torque, angular momentum, moment of inertia) is interleaved with a foreign 'chapter 13' whose Examples 13.1-13.8 and Figs 13.1-13.21 are clearly Oscillations/SHM ('A body oscillates with SHM', 'Two identical springs', 'length of a pendulum'). Two SUMMARY blocks and two POINTS TO PONDER blocks are present (one per chapter). 27 of 126 chunks are content_type=diagram. The 6.x and 13.x namespaces are separable by number, so native vs foreign counts CAN be split here (unlike the maths chapter).

headings.notes: Native ch6 numbered sections (6.1 Introduction ... ~6.7/6.8: Centre of mass, Motion of COM, Linear momentum, Vector product, Angular velocity, Torque & angular momentum, Equilibrium of rigid body, Moment of inertia, Rolling motion). ~8-9 native sections. Section-heading lines are partly buried in prose; medium confidence.

evidence: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
topics.notes: topic metadata uniformly correct for native chunks. High confidence on value (but foreign ch13 chunks also carry this topic label - metadata does not flag the contamination).

evidence: systems of particles and rotational motion 9 3
concepts.notes: 34 distinct concept-metadata values, but they are running page-header OCR ('9 4 physics', 'systems of particles and rotational motion 105', ...) - noise, not concepts. Low confidence as a content signal.

evidence: momentum of the system is defined to be the
angular momentum ... is defined as the vector
definitions.notes: ~34 raw 'is defined as/defined to be/we define' matches; ~20 distinct native definitions after dedup (centre of mass, linear momentum of system, torque, angular momentum, moment of inertia, radius of gyration, angular velocity, scalar/vector product...). Formal 'is defined as' phrasing is genuine here. Medium confidence.

formulae.notes: ~30 distinct NATIVE numbered equations (6.1, 6.2, 6.6-6.23, 6.27, 6.33-6.44) captured. A further ~26 foreign numbered equations (13.1-13.26) from the Oscillations contaminant are ALSO present but excluded from this native count. Plus many inline (un-numbered) expressions. Medium-high confidence for native numbered eqns.

evidence: Example 6.1 Find the centre of mass of
Example 6.12 A cord of negligible mass is
examples.notes: 12 distinct NATIVE worked examples Example 6.1 through 6.12, contiguous. Foreign Example 13.1-13.8 (Oscillations) are also present but excluded. High confidence for native 12.

evidence: Example 6.5 Find the torque of a force
solved_examples.notes: All 12 native examples are fully worked (given/find/solution). solved_examples == examples for native ch6. High confidence.

exercises.notes: Native 'EXERCISES' block present (markers 'exercises', 'exercises 6.1'). NCERT ch6 has ~33 end-of-chapter exercises; individual question stems are truncated/split across chunks so an exact question count is unreliable. Foreign 'exercise 13.9' marker also present. count=1 native exercise-block. Low confidence on question count.

activities.notes: No 'Activity N.M' blocks (Class 11 physics uses Examples/Exercises). High confidence (0).

tables.notes: 2 distinct native tables (Table 6.1 Moments of inertia..., Table 6.2). A foreign Table 13.1 is also present (excluded). High confidence for native 2.

diagrams.notes: ~33 distinct NATIVE figure numbers Fig. 6.1 through 6.33 (with sub-parts a/b and a few gaps e.g. 6.35 not seen). A further ~21 foreign figures (Fig. 13.1-13.21, Oscillations) are present but excluded. High confidence for native distinct-number count.

captions.notes: Roughly 25 native figures carry captions; several are bare references or split by OCR (e.g. 'fig.6.5(b)' vs 'Fig. 6.5 (b)'). Medium confidence.

summary.notes: Native 'SUMMARY' block present (there are TWO SUMMARY blocks in the chunks - one native ch6 at ~line 5814, one foreign ch13 at ~4052). present=yes. Also a native 'POINTS TO PONDER' block. High confidence on presence, medium on attributing which is native.

keywords.notes: NCERT physics has no Keywords block. High confidence (0).

suspected_missing: Foreign 'chapter 13' Oscillations/SHM content (Examples 13.1-13.8, Figs 13.1-13.21, a second SUMMARY + POINTS TO PONDER) is mis-merged into this ch6 row and should be removed/relocated.
End-of-chapter EXERCISES question stems are truncated/split - full ~33-question set not cleanly recoverable.
Figure sequence has gaps (e.g. Fig 6.35 not observed between 6.34 and 6.36) - possible mid-chapter chunk loss.

chapters.source_book: NCERT Class 12 Chemistry ch1 'Solutions' (NOT 'The Solid State') + foreign chapter numbered 6 content
chapters.contamination: METADATA DRIFT + MODERATE cross-chapter merge. The syllabus title says 'The Solid State' but the ACTUAL stored content is 'Solutions' (running header 'Solutions', section '1.1 Types of Solutions', Table 1.1 'Types of Solutions', Henry's law, Raoult's law, colligative properties, Intext Questions). In the rationalised NCERT (2023+) 'The Solid State' was DELETED and ch1 became 'Solutions' - so the title is stale. Additionally, foreign 'chapter 6' items (Figs 6.1-6.7, Examples 6.1-6.9, Tables 6.1-6.4) are interleaved. 15 of 97 chunks are content_type=diagram. The 1.x (Solutions) vs 6.x namespaces are separable by number.

headings.notes: Native Solutions sections 1.1 Types of Solutions, 1.2 Expressing Concentration, 1.3 Solubility, 1.4 Vapour Pressure (Raoult), 1.5 Ideal/Non-ideal, 1.6 Colligative Properties, 1.7 Abnormal Molar Mass (~7 native sections). Foreign '6.1.1' sub-section also present. Medium confidence.

topics.notes: topic metadata column says 'The Solid State' on every chunk - but the content is Solutions. This is the clearest METADATA DRIFT case: the label is wrong for 100% of chunks. Value high-confidence-captured, but semantically incorrect.

subtopics.notes: Equals native Solutions sections. Medium confidence.

concepts.notes: 30 distinct concept-metadata values, but they are running page-header OCR alternating '2 Chemistry' / '3 Solutions' / '4 Chemistry' ... - the header literally reads 'Solutions', further confirming the content identity. Noise as a concept count. Low confidence (but strong corroboration of the Solutions identity).

evidence: ...is defined as the number of moles of solute per litre of solution
definitions.notes: ~90 raw 'is defined as/is called/known as' matches (highest of the 5 chapters); ~40 distinct native definitions after dedup (molarity, molality, mole fraction, mass %, ppm, Henry's law, Raoult's law, ideal solution, colligative property, osmotic pressure, van't Hoff factor, azeotrope, etc.). Genuine formal definitions dense in this chapter. Medium confidence (raw count inflated by overlap).

formulae.notes: ~42 distinct NATIVE numbered equations (1.1-1.42) captured (molarity, molality, Raoult, relative lowering, elevation/depression, osmotic pressure, van't Hoff factor). A few stray (2.x) numbers also appear (likely OCR/foreign). Medium-high confidence for native numbered eqns.

examples.notes: 13 distinct NATIVE worked examples Example 1.1 through 1.13, contiguous. Foreign Example 6.1-6.9 also present but excluded. High confidence for native 13.

solved_examples.notes: All 13 native examples are fully worked with solution steps. solved_examples == examples. High confidence.

exercises.notes: Native 'Exercises' block present, plus multiple 'Intext Questions' blocks (post-section questions - a distinct dimension in NCERT chemistry). NCERT Solutions has ~2 sets of Intext Questions + ~41 end-of-chapter exercises; question stems truncated across chunks so exact count unreliable. count=1 exercise-block (+ Intext Questions present). Low confidence on question count.

activities.notes: No 'Activity N.M' blocks (Class 12 chemistry). Pre-scan matched 'activity 1.2' once but that is an OCR artifact, not a real Activity block. High confidence (0 real activities).

tables.notes: 4 distinct NATIVE tables Table 1.1-1.4. Foreign Table 6.1-6.4 also present but excluded. High confidence for native 4.

diagrams.notes: 11 distinct NATIVE figure numbers Fig. 1.1 through 1.11 (with a/b sub-parts). Foreign Fig. 6.1-6.7 also present but excluded. High confidence for native distinct-number count.

captions.notes: ~8 native figures carry captions; several bare references. Medium confidence.

summary.notes: Native 'Summary' block present (summary_marker=15 in pre-scan reflects heavy overlap duplication of the one Summary section, not 15 summaries). present=yes. Medium confidence on the single-instance attribution.

keywords.notes: NCERT chemistry has no Keywords block. High confidence (0).

suspected_missing: chapter_title 'The Solid State' is STALE/WRONG - actual content is 'Solutions' (rationalised NCERT deleted Solid State). Metadata must be corrected.
Foreign 'chapter 6' items (Figs 6.1-6.7, Examples 6.1-6.9, Tables 6.1-6.4) are mis-merged and should be relocated.
End-of-chapter Exercises and second Intext Questions set are truncated - full question sets not cleanly recoverable.
